name: AXS Delayed Retest Continuation v1
edge_mechanism: delayed entry after breakout retest holds
thesis: AXS 4h is the structurally different lane for testing delayed retest continuation: enter after the breakout proves it can hold, not on the first impulse. This should be denser and less fragile than immediate trigger logic.
invalidation_condition: Abort if retest-hold entries are still too sparse, if AXS only prints impulsive spikes with no orderly retest, or if held retests fail to extend.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A breakout occurs, price revisits the break zone without losing acceptance, and the continuation entry is taken on the resumed push rather than the original breakout bar.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Delayed retests often clean up breakout quality by letting weak breakouts fail before entry, especially when trend confidence is present but not dominant.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: AXS 4h is useful because it stress-tests whether the retest grammar can survive on a structurally different, thinner high-beta asset.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If AXS only gaps through levels or mean reverts too hard, orderly retests will not exist often enough.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If retests hold but the second leg is short, reduce runner ambition before increasing entry strictness.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Moderate hit rate with better average trade quality than immediate breakout entries.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: AXS plus one cleaner lane show tradable retest holds with enough density and post-cost expansion.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, chaotic spikes with no retest structure, or repeated holds that fail to extend.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad retest-hold conditions instead of exact candle timing

validation_basket.why_included: Stress-test lane for whether delayed retest continuation survives outside the majors.
validation_basket.continue_if: Retest holds print with enough density and meaningful continuation.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: No orderly retest structure appears or density collapses.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: A small number of high-quality retest holds should outperform immediate breakout chasing.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same delayed-entry continuation logic works on another volatile lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO shows comparable retest-hold behavior with better amplitude.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The pattern appears too asset-specific to AXS.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: More violent but still recognizable retest continuations.

validation_basket.why_included: Determines whether the retest grammar generalizes to a cleaner market.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH still prints delayed retests with orderly follow-through.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Only high-beta names show the pattern.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Cleaner but smaller continuation legs after held retests.
